title: The Scam of Illusion (Maya) | HG Amitasana Dasa
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Maya's trap is, Maya's scam is which Krishna busts in the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna says that
this very pleasure, this very pleasure, anyone would like to help me with the second line?
The scam is, Maya fools us that this very pleasure which we
so
emphatically, enthusiastically seek, hoping that it will make me happy, that very pleasure
becomes the cause of my misery. Now, here is a crude example you can relate with.
Bhagavatam gives this example of a fish. Now, you see the fisherman, when he goes to catch a fish,
what does he do on that fishing hook? He puts a bait and ensures that bait,
is a worm which is very, very tempting for the fish to eat. Now, fish gets tempted, it wants happiness,
it is seeking happiness, it goes for that bait and that very pleasure which the fish is seeking
becomes the cause of death of that fish. Today, if we are in a messy situation in life,
if we do root cause analysis, work backwards, if I am entangled,
hum Maya jaal mein phasay huay hain, if you work backwards, kaise phasay main, how did
I fail, how did I get entangled, because we moved with that understanding, just like that
fish, yeh mil jaye, I will enjoy like that, I will be very very happy, I will be very
very excited and that very excitement, that very happiness became the cause of my entanglement.
agar hum log aaj phasay huay hain, some people can realize, we are all stuck, we are
all entangled, phasay huay, Maya jaal mein phasay huay, how many of us believe Maya jaal
mein phasay huay hain, please raise your hands, soch rahe hain log, karey nahi karey, you
have time after the class you can think, why, if you do the root cause analysis, it's because
of this understanding which makes us believe that, yes, this happiness I seek, with all
enthu, with all this thing I go for it, and I get entangled, yehi samrsparsha jaabhoga,
dukh yonahi evate, adyavantaya kaunteya, it has adi and anta, it is temporary, naate
ramate budha, budha means an intelligent person, ramate means delight, budha means intelligent
person does not delight in such pleasures.
Sukham atyantikam atyendriyam, nothing wrong about seeking Sukham, we all are pleasure
seeking beings, but real pleasure, what will completely satisfy us, which we all are actually
wanting to experience, is atyantikam, anta means ending, atyantikam means it is unending,
and atyendriyam.
Atyantikam.
Atyantikam.
Atyantikam.
Atyantikam.
That which we will experience from our transcendental senses, not material senses.
